stories.title: Inside The Hazmat Incident That Disrupted The Pentagon
stories.summary:
Inside The Hazmat Incident That Disrupted The Pentagon. A hazardous materials incident that triggered a shelter-in-place order and emergency response at the Pentagon on Thursday has been resolved after officials determined there was no threat.

“Earlier this morning, Pentagon occupants were notified of a potential air quality issue, prompting immediate precautionary safety measures and evaluation,” Chief Pentagon Spokesman Sean Parnell said in an ... Al Jazeera: All clear given at Pentagon following after ‘hazardous materials’ alert.

Al Jazeera reports that department of Defense spokesman says 'shelter-in-place order' lifted after air quality issue detected.

stories.title: Deadly Sudan drone strike targets funeral procession
stories.summary:
A drone strike in the central Sudanese city of el-Obeid has killed up to 23 people, according to a report by Al Jazeera. The attack targeted a funeral procession, marking another violent incident in the ongoing civil war.

Local rights group Emergency Lawyers attributed the strike to the Rapid Support Forces (RSF) paramilitary group. As of now, the RSF has not immediately claimed responsibility for the deadly event.

links.headline: Drone strikes on central Sudanese city kill up to 23: NGO
links.excerpt: Local rights group Emergency Lawyers blamed the attack on the RSF, which did not immediately claim responsibility.
links.imageAlt: Drone strikes on central Sudanese city kill up to 23: NGO

stories.title: Top secrets come spilling out
stories.summary:
Top secrets come spilling out.

In 1917, British analysts deciphered a coded message the German foreign minister sent to one of his country's diplomats vowing to begin "unrestricted submarine warfare" and seeking to win over Mexico with a promise to "reconquer the lost territory in Texas, New Mexico and Arizona" if the US entered the world war.

When it became public, the Zimmerman Telegram caused a sensation, helping propel the US into the conflict against Germany.

When it became public, the Zimmerman Telegram caused a sensation, helping propel the US into the conflict against Germany.

stories.title: Child detainees in Queensland allegedly locked down for entire weekend
stories.summary:
Children held at two youth detention facilities in south-east Queensland were reportedly locked down for an entire weekend. The lockdown was attributed to severe staff shortages that have triggered a walk-off by dozens of correctional workers across the state.

The industrial action highlights ongoing staffing crises within the Queensland youth justice system. Authorities face mounting pressure as operational disruptions continue to impact the daily management and supervision of incarcerated minors.

links.headline: Child detainees in Queensland allegedly locked down for entire weekend
links.excerpt: Children incarcerated at two south-east Queensland youth detention facilities were allegedly locked down for an entire weekend due to staff shortages that have prompted dozens of workers across the state to walk off the job.

stories.title: Pool’s new app turns your screenshots into something useful
stories.summary:
Pool has launched a new application designed to transform the way users manage their digital screenshots. The app automatically organizes captured images into personalized collections, eliminating the need for manual sorting.

Beyond simple storage, Pool tracks down the original web links associated with saved content. This feature allows users to easily rediscover products, recipes, travel ideas, and other items they intended to revisit but had previously lost track of.

links.headline: Pool’s new app turns your screenshots into something useful
links.excerpt: Pool's new app automatically sorts screenshots into personalized collections, tracks down the original links behind saved content, and helps you rediscover products, recipes, travel ideas, and other things you meant to revisit.

AI is wonderful. It will break the world.

stories.title: MSU-GenSan seeks gov’t help to rise from quake
stories.summary:
Mindanao State University-General Santos City has requested immediate assistance from the national government following significant damage caused by a recent earthquake. The university estimates that infrastructure repairs will cost approximately 900 million pesos.

Chancellor Shidik Zed Abantas noted that current engineering assessments are preliminary and likely to increase as structural evaluations continue across campus facilities.

stories.title: Ateneo opens independent probe, seeks ‘absolute truth’ in fatal drowning
stories.summary:
Ateneo opens independent probe, seeks ‘absolute truth’ in fatal drowning. MANILA, Philippines — The Ateneo de Manila University’s Board of Trustees ordered an independent fact-finding investigation to ensure an impartial, thorough and careful review of the circumstances surrounding the deaths of student-athletes Rene Clert Baterbonia and Chukwuemeka Divine Adili.

stories.title: PCSO sends large-scale aid, vehicle fleet to quake-hit Mindanao
stories.summary:
The Philippine Charity Sweepstakes Office (PCSO) has mobilized a significant relief operation in Southern Mindanao following recent earthquakes. The agency dispatched essential supplies and a fleet of patient transport vehicles to the affected region on Thursday.

This deployment aligns with President Ferdinand Marcos Jr.'s directive for a swift and comprehensive government response to the disaster. The PCSO's latest effort follows its initial aid distribution, marking an escalation in support for the quake-stricken communities.

links.headline: PCSO sends large-scale aid, vehicle fleet to quake-hit Mindanao

stories.title: 3 minors rescued in Zamboanga City anti-trafficking raid
stories.summary:
Three minors were rescued during a joint anti-trafficking operation in Barangay Tigbalabag, Zamboanga City. The raid took place on Wednesday and targeted human trafficking activities in the area.

The operation was led by the Zamboanga Sea-Based Anti-Trafficking Task Force (ZSBATTF) alongside the Women and Children Protection Center–Mindanao Field Unit (WCPC-MFU). Alfredo Jimenez, head of ZSBATTF, confirmed that authorities successfully intervened to save the children from potential exploitation.

stories.title: These are the countries moving to ban social media for children
stories.summary:
Australia has become the first nation to implement a ban on social media for children, with the regulation taking effect in late 2025. The policy is designed to mitigate various risks associated with online platforms, including cyberbullying, addiction, and exposure to predators.

This legislative move places Australia at the forefront of global efforts to regulate digital spaces for minors. Other countries are now considering similar measures as they evaluate the impact of social media on youth development and safety.

links.headline: These are the countries moving to ban social media for children
links.excerpt: Australia was the first country to issue a ban in late 2025, aiming to reduce the pressures and risks that young users may face on social media, including cyberbullying, social media addiction, and exposure to predators.

stories.title: I was addicted to my phone – but one screen time hack actually made a difference
stories.summary:
A recent personal account highlights a surprisingly effective method for reducing smartphone addiction, as detailed in a new article from The Guardian. The writer utilized Apple's Screen Time app to track usage and discovered they were spending approximately eight hours per week browsing platforms like Reddit.

This data-driven approach allowed the author to identify specific habits contributing to excessive screen time. By implementing a targeted hack based on these insights, the individual reported a meaningful reduction in daily phone use.

stories.title: AI wealth boom sending San Francisco home prices surging: ‘It’s ridiculous’
stories.summary:
A surge in artificial intelligence wealth is driving San Francisco home prices to unprecedented levels, according to recent reports. Employees at leading AI firms are realizing substantial financial gains following a wave of initial public offerings.

This influx of capital has intensified competition in the Bay Area’s already expensive housing market. The rapid appreciation of property values has drawn attention from observers who describe the current conditions as extreme.
